$schema: Investor Profile - Family Office Extended (Direct Real Estate, Tax-First)

cashOnCash.description: Annual cash flow / equity invested (pre-tax; after-tax version mandatory alongside)

stateLocal.note: Override with actual state + local combined rate for family domicile

costSegregation.typicalAccelerationBenefit: 15-40% of depreciable basis front-loaded to 5/7/15-year property

exchange1031.dealImplication: Flag seller unwillingness to accommodate 1031 timing as dealbreaker at LOI stage

benefitTiers.deferral: Capital gains deferred until 12/31/2026 or earlier sale
benefitTiers.stepUp: 10% basis step-up if held 5 years (pre-2022 investment only; track legislative updates)
benefitTiers.exclusion: Post-acquisition gains excluded if held 10+ years

installmentSale.benefits: Spread capital gains recognition across installment payment years; reduce peak-year tax bracket exposure
risks: Interest rate risk on deferred payments

familyLimitedPartnership.benefit: Valuation discount 15-35% for minority interests; estate/gift tax efficiency
familyLimitedPartnership.flag: Recommend FLP review at portfolio management phase if portfolio value threshold exceeded

irrevocableTrust.flag: Alert when portfolio appreciation creates estate tax exposure

charitableVehicles.applicability: Philanthropic intent or highly appreciated asset disposition

targetK1Count.note: K-1 count drives tax preparation complexity and cost

managementHoursPerMonth.description: Total management hours across all assets per month for family principals and staff (not third-party PM)

scoringDimensions: active renovation or lease-up in progress

scoringScale.low: 1-3 assets, single market, same property type, stabilized
scoringScale.medium: 4-7 assets, 1-2 markets, 1-2 property types, minor value-add
scoringScale.high: 8+ assets, 3+ markets, mixed property types, active value-add

managementComplexity.action: Flag for review and potential operating partner engagement if score exceeds threshold

selfManagementThreshold.note: Below 30 units, self-management may be feasible; above 30 units, third-party PM is typically more efficient

steppedUpBasisValue.description: Unrealized gain that would be eliminated at death via IRC 1014 stepped-up basis; key metric for hold vs sell decision

flagThreshold.action: Highlight hold-for-step-up vs sell-now tradeoff in disposition analysis

reviewTriggers: Portfolio value exceeds federal estate tax exemption
Asset appreciation rate > 10% per year sustained 3+ years
Family generational transition planned within 10 years

recommendedVehicles.activeManagement: FLP or LLC with tiered membership interests
recommendedVehicles.estatePlanning: IDGT for estate freeze; GRAT for appreciation transfer
recommendedVehicles.philanthropy: DAF for charitable giving with investment flexibility

dimensions: asset quality (durable cash flows, low obsolescence risk)
legal structure clarity (clean title, no encumbrances)
legacyQualityScore.description: Aggregate score measuring suitability of asset for multi-generational hold (10-30 year horizon)

holdPeriodBias.note: Disposition trigger thresholds are set higher than base family-office profile; prefer to hold through cycle unless estate or tax event justifies sale

knownMarketsOnly.definition: Markets where the family has: (a) prior investment history, (b) an established operating partner, OR (c) direct personal network presence
knownMarketsOnly.unknownMarketAction: Flag as relationship-gap risk; require operating partner identification before proceeding

sourcingChannels: repeat brokers with prior deal history
personalNetworkDeals.note: Cold-source deals receive lower confidence weight in deal scoring absent relationship validation

offMarketPreference.rationale: Avoids competitive bid processes that compress returns; leverages relationship advantage
offMarketPreference.onMarketThreshold: Only pursue marketed deals if pricing is below underwritten value AND relationship exists with seller or broker

orchestratorPhaseModifications.marketResearch: Add family-network market filter. Eliminate markets where family has no relationships or operating partners. Focus on 2-4 markets with deep conviction rather than broad MSA sweep.
orchestratorPhaseModifications.strategy: After-tax return is primary metric, not pre-tax IRR. Strategy memo must include after-tax IRR, after-tax equity multiple, and stepped-up basis analysis for long-hold scenarios.
orchestratorPhaseModifications.acquisition: Widen base hold period assumption to 10-20 years. Mandate cost segregation study for acquisitions >= $2M. Lower leverage ceiling to 60% LTV max pass. Flag 1031 eligibility from day one.
orchestratorPhaseModifications.assetManagement: Reduce reporting frequency to quarterly. Add annual tax planning review (cost seg updates, depreciation schedule, 1031 pipeline). Operational burden hours tracked monthly against 60hr/month hard cap.
orchestratorPhaseModifications.portfolio: Add entity count and K-1 complexity as primary portfolio metrics. Flag if K-1 count exceeds 7. Track stepped-up basis value across portfolio. Annual estate planning review trigger.
orchestratorPhaseModifications.disposition: 1031 exchange is default assumption. Installment sale as secondary option. Taxable sale requires explicit override with tax impact modeled and approved. Step-up basis hold analysis mandatory.
orchestratorPhaseModifications.fundManagement: Typically not applicable (direct ownership preferred). Co-invest vehicles may apply for specific deals exceeding single-asset capital constraints. If co-invest: avoid UBTI, prefer pass-through K-1 structures.

dealbreakers: Management-intensive without established operating partner in market
Market where family has no relationships or operating partners
K-1 complexity would push total portfolio K-1 count above 10
Asset type requiring specialized operational expertise family does not have
Opportunity zone deal with hold commitment < 10 years (forfeits exclusion benefit)
Entity structure with UBTI exposure for trust-held investments
